agent_outputs.raw: The use of blocked random windows is justified here because the team empirically verified near-zero residual autocorrelation at the 15-minute horizon after conditioning, and confirmed equivalent model rankings under both evaluation methods across four historical periods. The evaluation plan was pre-specified based on these checks. The claim is correctly limited to short-horizon nightly-retrained forecasting. No methodological flaw. VERDICT: defense_wins

Non-chronological splits in time-series are a valid concern in general but not in this specific case. The pre-analysis showing near-i.i.d. residuals at the 15-minute horizon, combined with the four-period empirical equivalence check between blocked-random and rolling-origin evaluation, provides empirical justification for the non-standard design. The claim is limited to the regime where this justification holds. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

The standard objection to blocked random windows in forecasting is that it can introduce look-ahead bias. The team addressed this directly: residual autocorrelation is near zero at the 15-minute horizon after conditioning, and four historical periods confirmed that blocked-random and rolling-origin evaluation produce equivalent model rankings. The evaluation plan pre-specified this design. The scope is correctly limited. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

synthesis: All three assessors find the evaluation valid. The empirical pre-analysis showing near-zero residual dependence and equivalent rankings under both methods constitutes the required justification for departing from chronological evaluation. ETD constraint: unanimous defense wins.
all_issues_raised: non-chronological split concern — resolved by residual analysis and four-period comparison

empirical_test.condition: Reproduce residual-dependence pre-analysis and four-period rolling-origin vs blocked-random MAPE/ranking comparison
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Residual dependence substantial at 15-minute horizon; model rankings differ between evaluation methods
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Near-zero residual dependence confirmed; rankings equivalent under both methods; pre-specification documented
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Average equivalence holds but extreme-weather windows differ under the two approaches
